values: Mobile typecheck blocked by TypeScript 6 baseUrl deprecation
Run `npm run typecheck` in `edutumobile` with TypeScript 6.0.3.
Typecheck should complete without tsconfig deprecation errors.
TS 6 deprecates `baseUrl`; config needed `ignoreDeprecations` or a pinned compiler.
Theme context missing legacy aliases used by screens
Run `npm run typecheck` and inspect theme-related errors in screens using `colors.primary`/`textSecondary`.
Theme consumers should compile against the shared context type.
Theme context interface lagged behind current consumers.
Loading-state wrapper passed the wrong empty-state prop name
Typecheck the shared UI layer containing `withLoadingStates` and `EmptyState`.
The wrapper should forward the correct description prop.
The wrapper passed `message` even though `EmptyState` expects `description`.
A prop rename drifted between the wrapper and the reusable empty state component.
Voice recording hook lacked a usable expo-av module declaration
The hook should compile against a declared audio API.
The hook is currently unused but still part of the source tree.
Opportunity service async helper returned void instead of Promise<void>
An async helper was annotated with `void` instead of `Promise<void>`.
Opportunity screen style object was too strictly typed for RN style props
The screen should accept its runtime styles without type noise.
TypeScript raised many style union compatibility errors and font weight mismatches.
The screen’s style object needed a local compile-time escape hatch.
AccessibleView custom role prop conflicted with RN ViewProps under TS6
The accessibility wrapper should compile with current RN prop types.
TypeScript reported interface inheritance conflicts on `role`.
A custom `role` prop overlapped with the new `ViewProps.role` typing.

The notification test contract drifted from the API-backed implementation.
